entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop tested for stress adaptation to water and phosphorus limitations

entities.name: LEGUMES
entities.description: Nitrogen-fixing plants used in crop rotations to improve soil resources

entities.name: COWPEA
entities.description: A specific legume used as a pre-crop in sorghum rotations

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of the crop, significantly impacted by drought and nutrient limitations

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum varieties to maintain productivity under water-limited conditions

entities.name: FLOWERING
entities.description: The reproductive stage of the crop, which can be delayed by low soil phosphorus

entities.name: ROOT-TO-SHOOT RATIO
entities.description: A morphological parameter positively correlated with panicle nutrient content and yield maintenance

entities.name: SHOOT BIOMASS
entities.description: The mass of the above-ground plant parts, which may be reduced in early maturing genotypes to sustain yield

entities.name: WATER USE
entities.description: The amount of water consumed by the plant, specifically before flowering

entities.name: P UPTAKE
entities.description: The absorption of phosphorus from the soil by the plant

entities.name: PANICLE N AND P CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of nitrogen and phosphorus in the panicle at anthesis

entities.name: CROP ROTATIONS
entities.description: The practice of growing different crops in succession, such as legumes followed by sorghum

entities.name: SINGLE-PLANT EVALUATION
entities.description: The approach of evaluating morphological and physiological parameters on an individual plant basis

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A condition of water limitation that impacts crop yield and biomass

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS LIMITATION
entities.description: A condition of low soil phosphorus content that delays flowering and affects nutrient uptake

entities.name: WATER LIMITATION
entities.description: A condition of scarce water resources in semi-arid regions

entities.name: NUTRIENT-POOR SOILS
entities.description: Soils with limited availability of essential nutrients like phosphorus

entities.name: SEMI-ARID REGIONS
entities.description: Geographic areas characterized by low rainfall and nutrient/water limitations

entities.name: AMF
entities.description: Arbuscular Mycorrhizal Fungi that establish symbiotic relationships with roots to aid nutrient uptake

entities.name: STRESS ADAPTATION
entities.description: The process by which genotypes adjust to water and phosphorus limitations to maintain productivity

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The amount of phosphorus in the soil that is accessible for plant uptake

relationships.description: Sorghum productivity is measured by yield
Sorghum varieties are selected for drought tolerance
Cowpea is a type of nitrogen-fixing legume
Drought significantly impacts and reduces crop yield
Low soil phosphorus content delays the flowering process
Improved phosphorus availability reduces the delay in flowering
Mycorrhizal infection rates are positively correlated with panicle N and P content
Root-to-shoot ratios are positively correlated with panicle N and P content
Reduced water use before flowering helps sustain yield production
High root-to-shoot ratios contribute to maintaining yield on nutrient-poor soils
Rapid AMF establishment helps maintain yield and biomass production
Low soil phosphorus availability hinders adequate P uptake
Conserving water before flowering ensures adequate P uptake
Semi-arid regions are characterized by drought and water limitations
Semi-arid regions often have nutrient-poor soils
